title: Arrays, Pointers, Arithmetic, and Bounds · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar arrays, pointers, arithmetic, and bounds decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded image-slice library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.
Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Array Pointer Distinction
steps.instruction:
Order the Array Pointer Distinction evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Array Pointer Distinction.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Array Pointer Distinction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Array Pointer Distinction, keep Array Pointer Distinction connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 6870. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a half-open array slice before access; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Array Pointer Distinction has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
